reviews.text: Had the raya haji buffet n it was great! Nasi mandy nasi putih w dishes shabu2 grill lemang rendang kambing golek rojak eskrim - all my fav buffet things at reasonable rates. Staff were also nice and attentive. My new happy spot will def come again!

reviews.author: Quraisyah Zahariah
reviews.text:
I visited Volcano Shabu Shabu in Cyberjaya with my colleagues during lunch hour. There was no queue, which was a great start. The presentation of the restaurant was impressive, reminiscent of a hotel buffet. Its an all-you-can-eat steamboat experience, offering a variety of options including beef, lamb, fish, chicken, seafood, vegetables, and frozen items like fish balls, crab sticks n others.

Apart from the hotpot ingredients, they also served dim sum and sushi. Surprisingly, I enjoyed the sushi more than expected-perhaps because I was really hungry, but it genuinely tasted great. We added the grill option for RM9 per person, and the best part was that it was unlimited.

Overall, it was an enjoyable lunch session, allowing us to savor good food while chatting and relaxing with colleagues. The variety and unlimited options make it a great choice for a casual group outing.

reviews.author: idham sahrom
reviews.text: Wide variety of choices for food,  but not so much for drinks. Taste wise it was not very good, the chicken stir fry  was bland but it is to be expected in a all you can eat setting. The tomyum was just paste or cubed flavouring, no additional greenery or something to enhance the flavour. Service was quick but one of the waiter told us 2 out of 4 choice of meat is not available although we saw others eating them, luckily other waiter tender to us right after. For the price, not worth it but it was an okay experience.

reviews.author: Atiqah Ghazali-alKashif
reviews.text:
Mr Habibi wanted to try different kind of Chinese food that I don't really know how to cook, thus we came here.

We got salted egg yolk chicken and rice for me, ginger chicken and rice, and Kam Heong beef and rice for him. Yeap, he ate both because he was superbly famished. Ordered the mango sago as dessert too.

Everything was delicious, yet the salted egg yolk could be improved little bit more. The gravy was tad too thick for my liking, but I finished it to the last drop. Some of the sago in mango sago wasn't fully cooked, thus the opaque hue on it.

Altogether, our bill was less than RM70 which is relatively affordable. Surely we would come again as I need to let Mr Habibi try the yam ring cake. InsyaAllah soon. Thank you for serving us!

reviews.author: Feninferina Azman
reviews.text:
It's not my first time at Homst, but i probably had a so so experience at a different outlet. We decided to give it another shot, and this time around for buka puasa. It was jammed pack but we came around 8.00pm so it was okay. We placed our order and mentally prepared ourselves that the food will be slightly late. The crew were very attentive, worked fast, and did their best to deliver the food ASAP even though we heard that more rice was in demand and they were waiting for the rice to cook. The food selection is so satisfying. Very well organised menu, and they have absolutely almost everything that you want in a Chinese restaurant. We went for seafood and we were not disappointed. Love the butter prawns, not too oily, not too sweet, it was just delicious and fresh. The portion was really good for a regular sized plate, we got a total of 9 large prawns. Next the Kamheong lala. I just love the flavors that pops in, does not overshadowed by the prawns, they just go so well together. The kids had plain omelette and we ordered large, it was very large and delicious. Then for veg, we ordered salted fish baby kailan. You ca actually get the salted fish, it was just on point. Rice was rather small portion, but the quality of rice is really good. Suitable with the Chinese dishes. So even if you are dining with kids, you might want to order rice for each person.
Overall, enjoyed our iftar, will be back to try out more dishes!

reviews.author: MOHD AFIQ NAWI
reviews.text: The Lamb Shank was tender and flavorful, definitely the highlight. Lamb Kabsah was good too, though the meat was a bit dry. Loved the fresh mint tea and the warm, cheesy Kunafa. I think this is a real kunafa. The syrup also not too sweet. I can pour all of it onto kunafa.

reviews.author: Mel C ()
reviews.text:
Come here for Lamb Mandy, Lamb Haneeth, and Lamb Kabsa. Ive tried elsewhere and many other places, but still love the ones being served here.

For other dishes, I can only say that they are average. So, my only recommendations are the lamb dishes with rice.

Now, its all self order with QR code, at the table itself. All to be paid online upon ordering.

If you would like to pay cash, please pay first at the counter. Only then the order will be processed.

Service was good, and the young man assisted us to pack our food to-go since we couldnt finish it.

When we left, he thanked us and that it was a good thing because he was attentive to his job. Many places dont practice this anymore.

reviews.author: Hazwan A.K.
reviews.text:
Saba Restaurant in Cyberjaya is arguably one of the most well-known destinations for authentic Arab cuisine in the area. The food here is flavorful and well-prepared; we enjoyed an assortment of dishes, including chicken and beef kebabs, and the highlight of the meal, Nasi Mandy Talam. We also tried their Arab tea and Arab teh tarik, which complemented the meal perfectly.

Although we waited nearly 20 minutes for our order to arrive, the food was worth the wait. The staff, while not overly friendly, were efficient and well-informed about the menu and dishes. Its worth noting that the ordering system relies on QR codes, which may be slightly challenging, especially with the weak Celcom internet signal inside the restaurant. However, the restaurants atmosphere is vibrant, and it adds to the experience of dining there.

In summary, Saba Restaurant is a wonderful choice for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Arab dishes in Cyberjaya. Despite minor inconveniences, the quality of the food and the overall experience make this restaurant highly recommended for both locals and visitors.

reviews.author: Tengku Posh
reviews.text:
Last Saturday, we had lunch at Cyberview Resort. Since the verandah was reserved for a private event, the F&B manager kindly suggested we move to the Chinese restaurant. We had hoped to enjoy the dim sum buffet, but it was only available as a set menu, so we opted for the set menu for two at RM88+.

Sadly, the quality of the food didnt quite meet our expectations. The dim sum seemed like standard frozen varieties, lacking the special touch we remember. Additionally, the food delivery from the kitchen appeared to take longer, likely due to the restaurants new location.

We hope the resort will consider bringing back the dim sum buffet, as we really loved the variety. The peaceful and serene ambiance remains a highlight, and as a Cyberian, we feel fortunate to have such a wonderful resort just around the corner.

reviews.author: Razin Zaidei
reviews.text:
One of the best Johor style briyani/beriani sets you can get. If you are looking for:

- fragrant, fluffy, flavourful rice
- tender meats cooked in well balanced gravy (thick, not too sweet, great spices fragrance and aftertaste)
-tak kedekut amount of rice and meat - just ask for extra rice (you will find out why and thank me later)
-wallet friendly price at exceptional value and taste

This is one of those hidden gems - if you are a fans of Johor/Batu Pahat style Beriani, you owe it to yourself to eat here.

reviews.author: Azim Hulaimi

The rice itself was delicious, and the lauk they have which are ayam, daging and kambing, I ordered the kambing a bit and ayam masak merah which they also have. The chicken was very tasty and the kambing was super soft. Overall, a very satisfying lunch and I don't mind coming again and try other lauk. Price I would say reasonable with the portion they gave and how freshly cooked it is. Highly recommended restaurant if craving for Briyani batu pahat style

reviews.author: Mo Sopi
reviews.text:
The best nasi briyani in Putra Perdana 

**Atmosphere 5/5**
Can dine-in or take away. I find the restaurant to be clean and comfortable.
**Food 4/5**
So far this is the recommended nasi briyani johor in Puchong area. Although it doesn't taste exactly like the traditional Nasi Briyani Johor, it's still quite acceptable. Anyway, It's a bit spicy anyway. The drinks can be refilled for an additional RM1 each. The restaurant accept cash and QR.
**Parking 5/5**
Free street parking next to the restaurant. However, the nearby Sekolah Agama turns our the street into a cramped zone during rush hours.

reviews.author: mohammed taha
reviews.text:
We recently ordered from Nandos, and the experience was nothing short of appalling. To start with, we ordered two chicken chops, but they were replaced with sides. However, the most horrifying part was discovering a bug at the bottom of the rice, which is an absolute failure of basic hygiene standards.

Nandos is a reputed restaurant, and one would expect them to prioritise cleanliness and food safety. This incident has shattered that trust. We attempted to reach out to them to report the issue, but there was no response. Eventually, we managed to connect with Grabs customer service, only to be met with further disappointment. Instead of offering a full refund as a gesture of accountability and respect for hygiene standards, they proposed refunding only the affected item. This approach completely disregards the fact that contamination can affect the entire meal, not just a single component.

When I asked the customer service representative if he would personally eat the contaminated food, his response was shockingly dismissive. He said he would eat it and suggested "thinking positively." Thinking positively until I fall ill from food poisoning? This lack of concern for customer safety and satisfaction is beyond disappointing.

As a customer who used to order quite often from Nandos , I expected better from them. This experience has left me questioning their commitment to hygiene, customer care, and accountability. I hope they address this serious issue and ensure no other customer has to go through such a nightmare.

reviews.author: Sophia Neff
reviews.text: The food is great and always good quality. We waited for 30 minutes before asking about our food as they were a bit busy and they hadnt even started preparing it. After the first ask one meal came but it took over 10 more minutes to receive a simple salad and small kids meal. Usually the food arrives fairly quickly but this time it took 40 minutes for simple meals. The staff did not seem too concerned and didnt say anything after we asked where our meals were. Overall the only reason Im giving any stars is for the food.

reviews.author: Asyraf Azizan
reviews.text:
Well, if you ever find yourself in Cyberjaya around noon with a hankering for something hearty, Nasi Kak Wok at KB Corner is where you ought to head. Its one of those gems where the food speaks for itself. The star of the show is their Nasi Kakwok - juicy, golden chicken with just the right amount of seasoning, paired with a spicy sambal thatll wake up your taste buds in the best way.

Theyve got the classics down pat, offering Nasi Berlauk Gulai Kawah and, of course, the famous Nasi Kakwok. If youre watching your budget, the regular chicken will set you back a modest RM 9.00, but if youre feeling extra hungry, you can double down on the chicken for RM 14.00. And let me tell you, its worth every penny. They also have a station for Mee Celup, if noodles are more your thing.

The food is served fresh and hot, and youll have to pay first before digging in, but thats no big deal. The only downside? Parking. Managed by MPS, its paid parking, and if youre not careful, a summon might be waiting on your windshield. Plus, during office hours, finding a spot can be a bit like searching for a needle in a haystack.

But parking troubles aside, the food here is well worth it. Nasi Kak Wok never disappoints, and once youve had a bite, youll find yourself coming back again and again.

reviews.author: mohammed taha
reviews.text:
We recently ordered from Nandos, and the experience was nothing short of appalling. To start with, we ordered two chicken chops, but they were replaced with sides. However, the most horrifying part was discovering a bug at the bottom of the rice, which is an absolute failure of basic hygiene standards.

Nandos is a reputed restaurant, and one would expect them to prioritise cleanliness and food safety. This incident has shattered that trust. We attempted to reach out to them to report the issue, but there was no response. Eventually, we managed to connect with Grabs customer service, only to be met with further disappointment. Instead of offering a full refund as a gesture of accountability and respect for hygiene standards, they proposed refunding only the affected item. This approach completely disregards the fact that contamination can affect the entire meal, not just a single component.

When I asked the customer service representative if he would personally eat the contaminated food, his response was shockingly dismissive. He said he would eat it and suggested "thinking positively." Thinking positively until I fall ill from food poisoning? This lack of concern for customer safety and satisfaction is beyond disappointing.

As a customer who used to order quite often from Nandos , I expected better from them. This experience has left me questioning their commitment to hygiene, customer care, and accountability. I hope they address this serious issue and ensure no other customer has to go through such a nightmare.

reviews.author: Sophia Neff
reviews.text: The food is great and always good quality. We waited for 30 minutes before asking about our food as they were a bit busy and they hadnt even started preparing it. After the first ask one meal came but it took over 10 more minutes to receive a simple salad and small kids meal. Usually the food arrives fairly quickly but this time it took 40 minutes for simple meals. The staff did not seem too concerned and didnt say anything after we asked where our meals were. Overall the only reason Im giving any stars is for the food.

reviews.author: Atiqah Ghazali-alKashif
reviews.text:
Mr Habibi wanted to try different kind of Chinese food that I don't really know how to cook, thus we came here.

We got salted egg yolk chicken and rice for me, ginger chicken and rice, and Kam Heong beef and rice for him. Yeap, he ate both because he was superbly famished. Ordered the mango sago as dessert too.

Everything was delicious, yet the salted egg yolk could be improved little bit more. The gravy was tad too thick for my liking, but I finished it to the last drop. Some of the sago in mango sago wasn't fully cooked, thus the opaque hue on it.

Altogether, our bill was less than RM70 which is relatively affordable. Surely we would come again as I need to let Mr Habibi try the yam ring cake. InsyaAllah soon. Thank you for serving us!

reviews.author: Feninferina Azman
reviews.text:
It's not my first time at Homst, but i probably had a so so experience at a different outlet. We decided to give it another shot, and this time around for buka puasa. It was jammed pack but we came around 8.00pm so it was okay. We placed our order and mentally prepared ourselves that the food will be slightly late. The crew were very attentive, worked fast, and did their best to deliver the food ASAP even though we heard that more rice was in demand and they were waiting for the rice to cook. The food selection is so satisfying. Very well organised menu, and they have absolutely almost everything that you want in a Chinese restaurant. We went for seafood and we were not disappointed. Love the butter prawns, not too oily, not too sweet, it was just delicious and fresh. The portion was really good for a regular sized plate, we got a total of 9 large prawns. Next the Kamheong lala. I just love the flavors that pops in, does not overshadowed by the prawns, they just go so well together. The kids had plain omelette and we ordered large, it was very large and delicious. Then for veg, we ordered salted fish baby kailan. You ca actually get the salted fish, it was just on point. Rice was rather small portion, but the quality of rice is really good. Suitable with the Chinese dishes. So even if you are dining with kids, you might want to order rice for each person.
Overall, enjoyed our iftar, will be back to try out more dishes!

reviews.author: Rachel Raine
reviews.text: The staff were very friendly and helpful! The taste is good and freshness is maintained. Overall food and service matches the pricing. Soup was on the house! We ordered so much food for two, some pizza and snacks had to be kept overnight for breakfast. Lol. Thank you again, for being so nice to us!

reviews.author: meor sufian
reviews.text:
I would like to express my dissatisfaction regarding my recent order. The pizza arrived late and in a very poor condition-it was cold and unappetizing. When asked, the rider mentioned that the delay was due to an insufficient number of delivery riders.

This is not the first time we, as customers, have experienced such issues. Its disappointing, especially when we are paying for both the food and delivery service. We hope the top management takes this seriously and implements proper measures to ensure better service moving forward.

Thank you.

reviews.author: Syed Muzani
reviews.text:
Over one hour late. Used to be that 30 mins came with a free coupon. Now not even an apology.

Ordered pepperoni and extra cheese, both with mozzarella cheese tarik. Ended up with this unsliced mess of a cheese pizza and classified chicken, with no cheese tarik crust.  From the photo, it looks like a stuffed crust, but really it's just a fat empty crust.

Ordered the cheese sticks. We paid extra for all the cheese. They gave half as much cheese as usual.

Can't call them to ask what's going on with the order. No email, only a feedback form that goes into the void. It's understandable if they ran out of cheese or something at night, but we already paid and they can't refund.

Absolute last time we order from this Domino's.

reviews.author: MOHD AFIQ NAWI
reviews.text: The Lamb Shank was tender and flavorful, definitely the highlight. Lamb Kabsah was good too, though the meat was a bit dry. Loved the fresh mint tea and the warm, cheesy Kunafa. I think this is a real kunafa. The syrup also not too sweet. I can pour all of it onto kunafa.

reviews.author: Mel C ()
reviews.text:
Come here for Lamb Mandy, Lamb Haneeth, and Lamb Kabsa. Ive tried elsewhere and many other places, but still love the ones being served here.

For other dishes, I can only say that they are average. So, my only recommendations are the lamb dishes with rice.

Now, its all self order with QR code, at the table itself. All to be paid online upon ordering.

If you would like to pay cash, please pay first at the counter. Only then the order will be processed.

Service was good, and the young man assisted us to pack our food to-go since we couldnt finish it.

When we left, he thanked us and that it was a good thing because he was attentive to his job. Many places dont practice this anymore.

reviews.author: Hazwan A.K.
reviews.text:
Saba Restaurant in Cyberjaya is arguably one of the most well-known destinations for authentic Arab cuisine in the area. The food here is flavorful and well-prepared; we enjoyed an assortment of dishes, including chicken and beef kebabs, and the highlight of the meal, Nasi Mandy Talam. We also tried their Arab tea and Arab teh tarik, which complemented the meal perfectly.

Although we waited nearly 20 minutes for our order to arrive, the food was worth the wait. The staff, while not overly friendly, were efficient and well-informed about the menu and dishes. Its worth noting that the ordering system relies on QR codes, which may be slightly challenging, especially with the weak Celcom internet signal inside the restaurant. However, the restaurants atmosphere is vibrant, and it adds to the experience of dining there.

In summary, Saba Restaurant is a wonderful choice for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Arab dishes in Cyberjaya. Despite minor inconveniences, the quality of the food and the overall experience make this restaurant highly recommended for both locals and visitors.

reviews.author: Asyraf Azizan
reviews.text:
Well, if you ever find yourself in Cyberjaya around noon with a hankering for something hearty, Nasi Kak Wok at KB Corner is where you ought to head. Its one of those gems where the food speaks for itself. The star of the show is their Nasi Kakwok - juicy, golden chicken with just the right amount of seasoning, paired with a spicy sambal thatll wake up your taste buds in the best way.

Theyve got the classics down pat, offering Nasi Berlauk Gulai Kawah and, of course, the famous Nasi Kakwok. If youre watching your budget, the regular chicken will set you back a modest RM 9.00, but if youre feeling extra hungry, you can double down on the chicken for RM 14.00. And let me tell you, its worth every penny. They also have a station for Mee Celup, if noodles are more your thing.

The food is served fresh and hot, and youll have to pay first before digging in, but thats no big deal. The only downside? Parking. Managed by MPS, its paid parking, and if youre not careful, a summon might be waiting on your windshield. Plus, during office hours, finding a spot can be a bit like searching for a needle in a haystack.

But parking troubles aside, the food here is well worth it. Nasi Kak Wok never disappoints, and once youve had a bite, youll find yourself coming back again and again.

reviews.author: Quraisyah Zahariah
reviews.text:
I visited Volcano Shabu Shabu in Cyberjaya with my colleagues during lunch hour. There was no queue, which was a great start. The presentation of the restaurant was impressive, reminiscent of a hotel buffet. Its an all-you-can-eat steamboat experience, offering a variety of options including beef, lamb, fish, chicken, seafood, vegetables, and frozen items like fish balls, crab sticks n others.

Apart from the hotpot ingredients, they also served dim sum and sushi. Surprisingly, I enjoyed the sushi more than expected-perhaps because I was really hungry, but it genuinely tasted great. We added the grill option for RM9 per person, and the best part was that it was unlimited.

Overall, it was an enjoyable lunch session, allowing us to savor good food while chatting and relaxing with colleagues. The variety and unlimited options make it a great choice for a casual group outing.

reviews.author: Razin Zaidei
reviews.text:
One of the best Johor style briyani/beriani sets you can get. If you are looking for:

- fragrant, fluffy, flavourful rice
- tender meats cooked in well balanced gravy (thick, not too sweet, great spices fragrance and aftertaste)
-tak kedekut amount of rice and meat - just ask for extra rice (you will find out why and thank me later)
-wallet friendly price at exceptional value and taste

This is one of those hidden gems - if you are a fans of Johor/Batu Pahat style Beriani, you owe it to yourself to eat here.

reviews.author: Azim Hulaimi
reviews.text: One of the best Briyani I've ever eaten in a long time. The rice itself was delicious, and the lauk they have which are ayam, daging and kambing, I ordered the kambing a bit and ayam masak merah which they also have. The chicken was very tasty and the kambing was super soft. Overall, a very satisfying lunch and I don't mind coming again and try other lauk. Price I would say reasonable with the portion they gave and how freshly cooked it is. Highly recommended restaurant if craving for Briyani batu pahat style

reviews.author: Mo Sopi
reviews.text:
The best nasi briyani in Putra Perdana 

**Atmosphere 5/5**
Can dine-in or take away. I find the restaurant to be clean and comfortable.
**Food 4/5**
So far this is the recommended nasi briyani johor in Puchong area. Although it doesn't taste exactly like the traditional Nasi Briyani Johor, it's still quite acceptable. Anyway, It's a bit spicy anyway. The drinks can be refilled for an additional RM1 each. The restaurant accept cash and QR.
**Parking 5/5**
Free street parking next to the restaurant. However, the nearby Sekolah Agama turns our the street into a cramped zone during rush hours.

reviews.text: Planned a birthday dinner for my bf, however it was pure disappointment. Food was overpriced for the quality. They were so stingy with the salmon on the pizza, u can't find pieces of salmon rather mashed salmon, the cod fish was badly done. Worse part is that I asked teice if they gave complimentary birthday cake and they said yea, in the end they billed me for it. When I checked with the hotel, they said it was suppose to be complimentary. The restaurant staff cannot understand English well. Overall, it really ruined the birthday dinner.

reviews.author: Jac Yim
reviews.text:
Brought my friend to Bistro Cascata to celebrate her birthday.

I pre-ordered my set meal and requested if the restaurant could do something special for my guest. Mr Ameerul, the manager of the restaurant and his team did an excellent job in setting up decorations, providing live birthday song and a cake with personalised birthday wordings.

My guest was totally thrilled and surprised by the well planned arrangements. My guest and I had an extremely good and unforgettable time at your restaurant.

Service was excellent, staff was courteous, polite and friendly. Food was delicious. Both my guest and I loved the 5 course meal from the beginning till the end.

Once again, thank you very much for the experience tonight.

This is a place I strongly recommend for those who want to spend a quiet dinner with a special one.

reviews.author: Tengku Posh
reviews.text:
Last Saturday, we had lunch at Cyberview Resort. Since the verandah was reserved for a private event, the F&B manager kindly suggested we move to the Chinese restaurant. We had hoped to enjoy the dim sum buffet, but it was only available as a set menu, so we opted for the set menu for two at RM88+.

Sadly, the quality of the food didnt quite meet our expectations. The dim sum seemed like standard frozen varieties, lacking the special touch we remember. Additionally, the food delivery from the kitchen appeared to take longer, likely due to the restaurants new location.

We hope the resort will consider bringing back the dim sum buffet, as we really loved the variety. The peaceful and serene ambiance remains a highlight, and as a Cyberian, we feel fortunate to have such a wonderful resort just around the corner.

reviews.author: Edison Mark
reviews.text:

The fried radish cake is too hard as well. The place is quite comfortable.

I love to go to Cyberjaya Lake Gardens on every Friday after Jumma Prayer just to enjoy the lake.

This is definitely my go-to Nasi Kandar spot in Selangor -you can never go wrong with it! Malaysians will know that a classic Nasi Kandar order typically includes nasi putih, kuah campur, ayam goreng, and sayur. And of course, dont forget the papadom for that perfect crunchy finish to your meal!

Service is always fast at any mamak restaurant but few places can match the bold, rich flavour of the kuah campur they serve here.

reviews.author: Quraisyah Zahariah
reviews.text:
I visited Volcano Shabu Shabu in Cyberjaya with my colleagues during lunch hour. There was no queue, which was a great start. The presentation of the restaurant was impressive, reminiscent of a hotel buffet. Its an all-you-can-eat steamboat experience, offering a variety of options including beef, lamb, fish, chicken, seafood, vegetables, and frozen items like fish balls, crab sticks n others.

Apart from the hotpot ingredients, they also served dim sum and sushi. Surprisingly, I enjoyed the sushi more than expected-perhaps because I was really hungry, but it genuinely tasted great. We added the grill option for RM9 per person, and the best part was that it was unlimited.

Overall, it was an enjoyable lunch session, allowing us to savor good food while chatting and relaxing with colleagues. The variety and unlimited options make it a great choice for a casual group outing.

reviews.author: idham sahrom
reviews.text: Wide variety of choices for food,  but not so much for drinks. Taste wise it was not very good, the chicken stir fry  was bland but it is to be expected in a all you can eat setting. The tomyum was just paste or cubed flavouring, no additional greenery or something to enhance the flavour. Service was quick but one of the waiter told us 2 out of 4 choice of meat is not available although we saw others eating them, luckily other waiter tender to us right after. For the price, not worth it but it was an okay experience.

We got salted egg yolk chicken and rice for me, ginger chicken and rice, and Kam Heong beef and rice for him. Yeap, he ate both because he was superbly famished. Ordered the mango sago as dessert too.

Everything was delicious, yet the salted egg yolk could be improved little bit more. The gravy was tad too thick for my liking, but I finished it to the last drop. Some of the sago in mango sago wasn't fully cooked, thus the opaque hue on it.

Altogether, our bill was less than RM70 which is relatively affordable. Surely we would come again as I need to let Mr Habibi try the yam ring cake. InsyaAllah soon. Thank you for serving us!

reviews.author: Feninferina Azman
reviews.text:
It's not my first time at Homst, but i probably had a so so experience at a different outlet. We decided to give it another shot, and this time around for buka puasa. It was jammed pack but we came around 8.00pm so it was okay. We placed our order and mentally prepared ourselves that the food will be slightly late. The crew were very attentive, worked fast, and did their best to deliver the food ASAP even though we heard that more rice was in demand and they were waiting for the rice to cook. The food selection is so satisfying. Very well organised menu, and they have absolutely almost everything that you want in a Chinese restaurant. We went for seafood and we were not disappointed. Love the butter prawns, not too oily, not too sweet, it was just delicious and fresh. The portion was really good for a regular sized plate, we got a total of 9 large prawns. Next the Kamheong lala. I just love the flavors that pops in, does not overshadowed by the prawns, they just go so well together. The kids had plain omelette and we ordered large, it was very large and delicious. Then for veg, we ordered salted fish baby kailan. You ca actually get the salted fish, it was just on point. Rice was rather small portion, but the quality of rice is really good. Suitable with the Chinese dishes. So even if you are dining with kids, you might want to order rice for each person.
Overall, enjoyed our iftar, will be back to try out more dishes!

reviews.author: Hazwan A.K.
reviews.text:
Saba Restaurant in Cyberjaya is arguably one of the most well-known destinations for authentic Arab cuisine in the area. The food here is flavorful and well-prepared; we enjoyed an assortment of dishes, including chicken and beef kebabs, and the highlight of the meal, Nasi Mandy Talam. We also tried their Arab tea and Arab teh tarik, which complemented the meal perfectly.

Although we waited nearly 20 minutes for our order to arrive, the food was worth the wait. The staff, while not overly friendly, were efficient and well-informed about the menu and dishes. Its worth noting that the ordering system relies on QR codes, which may be slightly challenging, especially with the weak Celcom internet signal inside the restaurant. However, the restaurants atmosphere is vibrant, and it adds to the experience of dining there.

In summary, Saba Restaurant is a wonderful choice for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Arab dishes in Cyberjaya. Despite minor inconveniences, the quality of the food and the overall experience make this restaurant highly recommended for both locals and visitors.

We had hoped to enjoy the dim sum buffet, but it was only available as a set menu, so we opted for the set menu for two at RM88+.

reviews.author: mohammed taha
reviews.text:
We recently ordered from Nandos, and the experience was nothing short of appalling. To start with, we ordered two chicken chops, but they were replaced with sides. However, the most horrifying part was discovering a bug at the bottom of the rice, which is an absolute failure of basic hygiene standards.

Nandos is a reputed restaurant, and one would expect them to prioritise cleanliness and food safety. This incident has shattered that trust. We attempted to reach out to them to report the issue, but there was no response. Eventually, we managed to connect with Grabs customer service, only to be met with further disappointment. Instead of offering a full refund as a gesture of accountability and respect for hygiene standards, they proposed refunding only the affected item. This approach completely disregards the fact that contamination can affect the entire meal, not just a single component.

When I asked the customer service representative if he would personally eat the contaminated food, his response was shockingly dismissive. He said he would eat it and suggested "thinking positively." Thinking positively until I fall ill from food poisoning? This lack of concern for customer safety and satisfaction is beyond disappointing.

As a customer who used to order quite often from Nandos , I expected better from them. This experience has left me questioning their commitment to hygiene, customer care, and accountability. I hope they address this serious issue and ensure no other customer has to go through such a nightmare.

After the first ask one meal came but it took over 10 more minutes to receive a simple salad and small kids meal. Usually the food arrives fairly quickly but this time it took 40 minutes for simple meals. The staff did not seem too concerned and didnt say anything after we asked where our meals were. Overall the only reason Im giving any stars is for the food.

reviews.author: Asyraf Azizan
reviews.text:
Well, if you ever find yourself in Cyberjaya around noon with a hankering for something hearty, Nasi Kak Wok at KB Corner is where you ought to head. Its one of those gems where the food speaks for itself. The star of the show is their Nasi Kakwok - juicy, golden chicken with just the right amount of seasoning, paired with a spicy sambal thatll wake up your taste buds in the best way.

Theyve got the classics down pat, offering Nasi Berlauk Gulai Kawah and, of course, the famous Nasi Kakwok. If youre watching your budget, the regular chicken will set you back a modest RM 9.00, but if youre feeling extra hungry, you can double down on the chicken for RM 14.00. And let me tell you, its worth every penny. They also have a station for Mee Celup, if noodles are more your thing.

The food is served fresh and hot, and youll have to pay first before digging in, but thats no big deal. The only downside? Parking. Managed by MPS, its paid parking, and if youre not careful, a summon might be waiting on your windshield. Plus, during office hours, finding a spot can be a bit like searching for a needle in a haystack.

But parking troubles aside, the food here is well worth it. Nasi Kak Wok never disappoints, and once youve had a bite, youll find yourself coming back again and again.

reviews.author: Atiqah Ghazali-alKashif
reviews.text:
Mr Habibi wanted to try different kind of Chinese food that I don't really know how to cook, thus we came here.

We got salted egg yolk chicken and rice for me, ginger chicken and rice, and Kam Heong beef and rice for him. Yeap, he ate both because he was superbly famished. Ordered the mango sago as dessert too.

Everything was delicious, yet the salted egg yolk could be improved little bit more. The gravy was tad too thick for my liking, but I finished it to the last drop. Some of the sago in mango sago wasn't fully cooked, thus the opaque hue on it.

Altogether, our bill was less than RM70 which is relatively affordable. Surely we would come again as I need to let Mr Habibi try the yam ring cake. InsyaAllah soon. Thank you for serving us!

reviews.author: Feninferina Azman
reviews.text:
It's not my first time at Homst, but i probably had a so so experience at a different outlet. We decided to give it another shot, and this time around for buka puasa. It was jammed pack but we came around 8.00pm so it was okay. We placed our order and mentally prepared ourselves that the food will be slightly late. The crew were very attentive, worked fast, and did their best to deliver the food ASAP even though we heard that more rice was in demand and they were waiting for the rice to cook. The food selection is so satisfying. Very well organised menu, and they have absolutely almost everything that you want in a Chinese restaurant. We went for seafood and we were not disappointed. Love the butter prawns, not too oily, not too sweet, it was just delicious and fresh. The portion was really good for a regular sized plate, we got a total of 9 large prawns. Next the Kamheong lala. I just love the flavors that pops in, does not overshadowed by the prawns, they just go so well together. The kids had plain omelette and we ordered large, it was very large and delicious. Then for veg, we ordered salted fish baby kailan. You ca actually get the salted fish, it was just on point. Rice was rather small portion, but the quality of rice is really good. Suitable with the Chinese dishes. So even if you are dining with kids, you might want to order rice for each person.
Overall, enjoyed our iftar, will be back to try out more dishes!

One of the best Johor style briyani/beriani sets you can get. If you are looking for:

- fragrant, fluffy, flavourful rice
- tender meats cooked in well balanced gravy (thick, not too sweet, great spices fragrance and aftertaste)
-tak kedekut amount of rice and meat - just ask for extra rice (you will find out why and thank me later)
-wallet friendly price at exceptional value and taste

This is one of those hidden gems - if you are a fans of Johor/Batu Pahat style Beriani, you owe it to yourself to eat here.

reviews.author: Quraisyah Zahariah
reviews.text:
I visited Volcano Shabu Shabu in Cyberjaya with my colleagues during lunch hour. There was no queue, which was a great start. The presentation of the restaurant was impressive, reminiscent of a hotel buffet. Its an all-you-can-eat steamboat experience, offering a variety of options including beef, lamb, fish, chicken, seafood, vegetables, and frozen items like fish balls, crab sticks n others.

Apart from the hotpot ingredients, they also served dim sum and sushi. Surprisingly, I enjoyed the sushi more than expected-perhaps because I was really hungry, but it genuinely tasted great. We added the grill option for RM9 per person, and the best part was that it was unlimited.

Overall, it was an enjoyable lunch session, allowing us to savor good food while chatting and relaxing with colleagues. The variety and unlimited options make it a great choice for a casual group outing.

Service was quick but one of the waiter told us 2 out of 4 choice of meat is not available although we saw others eating them, luckily other waiter tender to us right after. For the price, not worth it but it was an okay experience.

reviews.author: mohammed taha
reviews.text:
We recently ordered from Nandos, and the experience was nothing short of appalling. To start with, we ordered two chicken chops, but they were replaced with sides. However, the most horrifying part was discovering a bug at the bottom of the rice, which is an absolute failure of basic hygiene standards.

Nandos is a reputed restaurant, and one would expect them to prioritise cleanliness and food safety. This incident has shattered that trust. We attempted to reach out to them to report the issue, but there was no response. Eventually, we managed to connect with Grabs customer service, only to be met with further disappointment. Instead of offering a full refund as a gesture of accountability and respect for hygiene standards, they proposed refunding only the affected item. This approach completely disregards the fact that contamination can affect the entire meal, not just a single component.

When I asked the customer service representative if he would personally eat the contaminated food, his response was shockingly dismissive. He said he would eat it and suggested "thinking positively." Thinking positively until I fall ill from food poisoning? This lack of concern for customer safety and satisfaction is beyond disappointing.

As a customer who used to order quite often from Nandos , I expected better from them. This experience has left me questioning their commitment to hygiene, customer care, and accountability. I hope they address this serious issue and ensure no other customer has to go through such a nightmare.

reviews.author: Sophia Neff
reviews.text: The food is great and always good quality. We waited for 30 minutes before asking about our food as they were a bit busy and they hadnt even started preparing it. After the first ask one meal came but it took over 10 more minutes to receive a simple salad and small kids meal. Usually the food arrives fairly quickly but this time it took 40 minutes for simple meals. The staff did not seem too concerned and didnt say anything after we asked where our meals were. Overall the only reason Im giving any stars is for the food.

reviews.author: MOHD AFIQ NAWI
reviews.text: The Lamb Shank was tender and flavorful, definitely the highlight. Lamb Kabsah was good too, though the meat was a bit dry. Loved the fresh mint tea and the warm, cheesy Kunafa. I think this is a real kunafa. The syrup also not too sweet. I can pour all of it onto kunafa.

reviews.author: Mel C ()
reviews.text:
Come here for Lamb Mandy, Lamb Haneeth, and Lamb Kabsa. Ive tried elsewhere and many other places, but still love the ones being served here.

For other dishes, I can only say that they are average. So, my only recommendations are the lamb dishes with rice.

Now, its all self order with QR code, at the table itself. All to be paid online upon ordering.

If you would like to pay cash, please pay first at the counter. Only then the order will be processed.

Service was good, and the young man assisted us to pack our food to-go since we couldnt finish it.

When we left, he thanked us and that it was a good thing because he was attentive to his job. Many places dont practice this anymore.

reviews.author: Hazwan A.K.
reviews.text:
Saba Restaurant in Cyberjaya is arguably one of the most well-known destinations for authentic Arab cuisine in the area. The food here is flavorful and well-prepared; we enjoyed an assortment of dishes, including chicken and beef kebabs, and the highlight of the meal, Nasi Mandy Talam. We also tried their Arab tea and Arab teh tarik, which complemented the meal perfectly.

Although we waited nearly 20 minutes for our order to arrive, the food was worth the wait. The staff, while not overly friendly, were efficient and well-informed about the menu and dishes. Its worth noting that the ordering system relies on QR codes, which may be slightly challenging, especially with the weak Celcom internet signal inside the restaurant. However, the restaurants atmosphere is vibrant, and it adds to the experience of dining there.

In summary, Saba Restaurant is a wonderful choice for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Arab dishes in Cyberjaya. Despite minor inconveniences, the quality of the food and the overall experience make this restaurant highly recommended for both locals and visitors.

reviews.author: Asyraf Azizan
reviews.text:
Well, if you ever find yourself in Cyberjaya around noon with a hankering for something hearty, Nasi Kak Wok at KB Corner is where you ought to head. Its one of those gems where the food speaks for itself. The star of the show is their Nasi Kakwok - juicy, golden chicken with just the right amount of seasoning, paired with a spicy sambal thatll wake up your taste buds in the best way.

Theyve got the classics down pat, offering Nasi Berlauk Gulai Kawah and, of course, the famous Nasi Kakwok. If youre watching your budget, the regular chicken will set you back a modest RM 9.00, but if youre feeling extra hungry, you can double down on the chicken for RM 14.00. And let me tell you, its worth every penny. They also have a station for Mee Celup, if noodles are more your thing.

The food is served fresh and hot, and youll have to pay first before digging in, but thats no big deal. The only downside? Parking. Managed by MPS, its paid parking, and if youre not careful, a summon might be waiting on your windshield. Plus, during office hours, finding a spot can be a bit like searching for a needle in a haystack.

But parking troubles aside, the food here is well worth it. Nasi Kak Wok never disappoints, and once youve had a bite, youll find yourself coming back again and again.
